topic: Someone finds a wallet containing a large sum of cash
raw_output:
[story 1]

The worn leather of the wallet felt heavy in my palm, a tangible weight that settled uncomfortably in my chest. It wasn't just the crisp bills; it was the sheer volume of it, a stark contrast to the meager allowance I usually managed to scrape together. I traced the edges of the worn material, my gaze flicking towards the street, watching passersby with a tightening in my jaw. A knot of something sharp and unwelcome began to coil low in my stomach. This windfall felt undeserved, a cruel twist of fate aimed directly at me. I tucked it quickly into my bag, the sound of the paper rustling sounding deafeningly loud in the quiet corner store.

[story 2]

When I pulled the wallet out, the sheer thickness of the cash made my hands tremble slightly. Every line of the money seemed to mock my own financial constraints. I stood there by the counter, pretending to examine a misplaced receipt, but my eyes kept darting back to the thick wad of currency. A tightness constricted my throat, making it hard to draw a full breath. I watched the owner of the store, a man who seemed so effortlessly prosperous, and a hot, prickly feeling rose up, making my skin feel too tight. I quickly slid the wallet back across the counter, my voice coming out a little too high, a forced lightness masking the churning resentment inside.

[story 3]

The moment my fingers closed around the stack of banknotes, a sudden, hot flush spread across my neck and cheeks. It was an intense, burning sensation that made me pull my hand back almost immediately. I stared at the money, the colors of the bills seeming too bright, too abundant. My breath hitched, shallow and quick. I felt a sharp, unpleasant clenching in my chest, a feeling of being utterly excluded from something I desperately wanted. I quickly shoved the wallet deep into my coat pocket, the fabric rough against my skin, desperate to hide the undeniable proof of someone else's easy fortune.
